primary_accounting_issue: Possibilités de documentation de couverture en comptes consolidés pour le risque de change attaché à un dividende intragroupe (créance/dividende futur) et identification des modèles IFRS de comptabilité de couverture pertinents

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by first identifying the governing lineage for hedge accounting models and eligibility; IFRS 9, as an IFRS standard, directly governs hedge accounting and therefore takes precedence over IAS-based sources, while IAS 21 remains supporting because it explains the consolidation and foreign-exchange effects of intragroup monetary items rather than defining the hedge accounting models themselves.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low: IFRS 9 is the clear governing standard for hedge accounting, while IAS 21 is needed only to frame when intragroup foreign-exchange effects remain visible in consolidation.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern what can be designated as a hedged item in hedge accounting, including intragroup foreign-currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify that foreign-currency monetary items are retranslated and that exchange differences on intragroup monetary items may remain in consolidated profit or loss.
This paragraph helps frame when a dividend is recognised as a receivable, which is relevant to distinguishing a recognised monetary item from a forecast dividend.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address classification and presentation of dividends and equity/liability effects, not hedge accounting models for foreign-exchange risk in consolidation.
These paragraphs concern related-party disclosures and intragroup eliminations generally, but do not help identify hedge accounting approaches.
These paragraphs address tax effects of investments and distributions, not hedge accounting approaches for FX risk on intragroup dividends.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for a recognised foreign-currency monetary exposure in consolidated financial statements
Hedge accounting for a highly probable forecast foreign-currency intragroup transaction affecting consolidated profit or loss
Hedge accounting for foreign-currency exposure forming part of a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes fair value hedge accounting for recognised hedged items, which is a peer top-level model relevant to a recognised foreign-currency dividend receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes cash flow hedge accounting for highly probable forecast transactions, including eligible intragroup foreign-currency transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedging of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign-currency exposure.
